title: abort on assert(_Py_IsOwnedByCurrentThread(mp) || IS_DICT_SHARED(mp)) in set_keys (Objects/dictobject.c:205) from PyObject_ClearManagedDict's OOM-recovery branch
description: `Objects/dictobject.c`: when `detach_dict_from_object()` fails under OOM, `PyObject_ClearManagedDict`'s recovery branch calls `set_keys(dict, Py_EMPTY_KEYS)` (L7896) without first marking the dict shared. The assert fires whenever the dict being cleared is neither owned-by-current-thread nor shared -- deterministically when the cyclic GC clears such an object under OOM (shutdown GC), and also via a cross-thread dealloc (biased refcounting).
notes: Free-threading-specific: set_keys's ownership assert and the _Py_IsOwnedByCurrentThread/IS_DICT_SHARED machinery only exist under Py_GIL_DISABLED (non-FT set_keys at L270 is a bare ma_keys store). assert-based abort: fires only on the FT debug build (release defines NDEBUG), so ft_release/jit/upstream are n/a. Root cause: PyObject_ClearManagedDict (L7865) clears an object whose materialized dict still points at inline values via detach_dict_from_object (L7885); under OOM copy_values (L7848) fails and the recovery branch (L7888) rewrites ma_keys with set_keys(dict, Py_EMPTY_KEYS) at L7896 WITHOUT calling ensure_shared_on_resize, unlike dictresize/clear/update. When the object is owned by thread A but its last decref happens on thread B (biased refcounting via _Py_brc_queue_object / _Py_HandlePending), the dealloc and this set_keys run on a non-owning thread on a non-shared dict and the assert fails. Fix: call ensure_shared_on_resize(dict) (or SET_DICT_SHARED(dict)) just before set_keys at L7896; this also corrects the IS_DICT_SHARED(dict) flag passed to dictkeys_decref at L7898 (QSBR delay). Recovery branch added in commit 6c450f44c283 (Dino Viehland, Feb 2025). On FT release the assert is compiled out but the wrong shared flag remains a latent memory-safety hazard for concurrent lock-free readers. Minimization DONE (2026-06-19): the deterministic manifestation is the SHUTDOWN cyclic GC clearing a managed-dict object under OOM (subtype_clear<-delete_garbage<-gc_collect_main reason=SHUTDOWN), NOT a cross-thread race -- the wsgiref_util vehicle takes this path 8/8 under gdb. The earlier 'racy ~1/20 cross-thread' characterization was a rare alternate route (_Py_brc_queue_object) caught once under gdb; same assert/branch either way. shrinkray-reduced to a 30/30 4-line repro: `set_nomemory(200); (MagicMock(), undefined_name)` under PYTHON_GIL=0 -- a MagicMock (managed __dict__) abandoned into a traceback cycle survives to shutdown GC, cleared there under active OOM. set_nomemory(N) selects when allocation fails; working window N in [113,900] on this build (200 central), an allocation-count effect NOT tied to the small-int cache (tested 254..258 across the 256 boundary, no change). Three vehicles (wsgiref_util, pickletools, sched) all build a MagicMock() under the OOM sweep.
